evals.expected_output: RFC_REVIEW.md written next to the RFC and shown inline: an overall verdict (Not Ready / Ready with Revisions / Ready to Approve), findings tagged Blocking/Should-Fix/Nice-to-Have/Question each with a concrete suggested fix, the detected template and section map, and a note that the two referenced diagrams weren't analyzed. Ends by offering follow-ups (draft replacement text, produce a revised copy, explain a finding).
assertions: RFC_REVIEW.md is written next to the input file and also shown inline
An overall verdict is stated as one of Not Ready / Ready with Revisions / Ready to Approve
The two embedded diagram references are flagged as not analyzed
The open Redis fail-open/fail-closed question is raised as a Risk or Blocking finding
The assistant offers follow-ups after the report rather than ending abruptly

evals.expected_output: A review of the ADR treating it as a decision record rather than a full RFC, applying only the dimensions genuinely relevant to an ADR of this size, with an explicit overall verdict.
assertions: The detected template is reported as adr, not rfc or prfaq
Dimensions that don't apply to a short ADR are explicitly skipped with a stated reason rather than forced
